clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Prevent mild cognitive impairment and dementia through blood pressure control in hypertensive adults
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Adults with hypertension, cognitive health screening, dementia prevention counseling
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: SBP goal <130 mm Hg recommendation with COR 1 + LOE A classification

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for MCI/dementia prevention module

hasHypertension.validation: Must be true for this recommendation

validationRules: Patient must be adult with hypertension
SBP measurement required for goal tracking

rules: LOE A = High-quality evidence from >1 RCT or meta-analyses of high-quality RCTs
Target SBP <130 mm Hg for cognitive protection
Recommendation applies to primary and secondary prevention of MCI/dementia
Should be implemented in all adults with hypertension regardless of age

categories.name: SBP Goal Not Achieved
categories.action: Intensify antihypertensive therapy, reassess in 1-3 months

category.description: 'Prevention of Mild Cognitive Impairment and Dementia'

recommendation.description: In adults with hypertension, a goal of <130 mm Hg SBP is recommended to prevent mild cognitive impairment and dementia

suggestedPhrasing.description: Is recommended / Is indicated / Should be performed

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Implement SBP goal <130 mm Hg in all adults with hypertension
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Monitor BP every 3-6 months, assess cognition annually
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Confirm SBP <130 mm Hg at each visit
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag patients with SBP ≥130 mm Hg for treatment intensification
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log SBP readings and cognitive screening results to health record
